title: rhamnus lanceolata
intro: Rhamnus lanceolata, also known as Frangula purshiana or Cascara Sagrada, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to western North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative and to treat gastrointestinal issues due to its anthraquinone content. The health benefits of Rhamnus lanceolata include promoting regular bowel movements, relieving constipation, and supporting the digestive system. Herbal preparations of Rhamnus lanceolata include dried bark, capsules, and teas, often used to treat various gastrointestinal problems and support overall digestive health.
